titleStmt.title: Introduction to the site of East Mount Shuyuan

change.item: Abschnitt Related Monasteries eingefügt (vgl. Mount Dazhai)
Abschnitt Related Monasteries geändert auf Verweis auf Mount Tianchi.
Änderungen und Kürzungen von LL eingearbeitet

figure.head: Inscription bearing rock at the west side of East Mount Shuyuan, photograph taken in 2004
書院東山西麓石刻摩崖，2004年照片

div.head: 地理位置
div.p: 書院東山是魯中山脈的另一刻經處，位於大寨谷之東（）。
書院東山位於平陰縣西南25公里、聊城市東南偏南48公里、東平縣西北22公里處（見封二地圖）。山腳海拔高度約100米。目前，摩崖上没有現代人為開鑿痕跡。附近書院村的房屋緊鄰山腳。
書院東山的地理坐標是東經116.2937度，北緯36.0997度，相當於UTM坐標系統第50S帶，東行436420，北行3995240。石刻在書院東山西麓山腳上方僅幾米處，面向西南偏西方。書院東山北麓，與天池山相連。天池山南麓也有石刻，石刻面向西南方（地圖，）。書院東山上的石刻和山東其他石刻點之間的空間關係,見天池山的相關介紹。

div.head: 地形與空間構成
div.p: 書院東山僅有的一處石刻，位於東山西麓（），靠近村落的陡峭崖壁下方。

div.head: 釋名
div.p: 該山位於平陰縣洪範池鎮書院村以東，故村民稱之為“東山”。

div.head: 相關寺院
div.p: 見天池山相關部分。

div.head: 相關人物
div.p: 該遺址没有留下相關人物的信息。

div.head: 斷代
div.p: 除了佛名之外，没有其他題銘存在，因此很難對書院東山的石刻進行確切斷代。根據風格分析，佛名的書法樣式與山東境內其他北齊、北周時期刊刻的佛名相近。

figure.head: Detail of East Mount Shuyuan’s “Buddha King of Great Emptiness” inscription; photograph taken in 2006
書院東山刊刻的“大空王佛”（局部），2006年照片

div.head: 研究史
div.p: 2003年出版的《北朝摩崖刻經研究［續］》多次提及書院東山的石刻。 張總4；賴非104, 120；桐谷征一51, 74；田雄信之180。賴非2007年的專著《山東北朝佛教摩崖刻經調查與研究》，提供完整的銘刻信息。賴非 。

figure.head: Shuyuan Village, encircled by the East Mount and Mount Tianchi; photograph taken in 2004
東山和天池山環繞書院村，2006年照片

div.head: 銘文總數
div.p: 書院東山僅有一處佛名銘刻（書院東山1號）。無佛經節文與題記。

div.head: 書院東山1號
div.p: 關於“大空王佛”佛名的含義，見洪頂山相關介紹。有關此佛名在山東省境内刊刻次數，參照洪頂山8號的“討論”部分。

div.head: 刊刻技法
div.p: 技法主要是“Ｕ”形刻法，即順筆畫中心單鏨刻成，方便快捷。

div.head: 書法藝術
div.p: 在字與字之間以及字的筆畫周圍，可見事先打磨的痕跡。字跡呈北齊刻風。“佛”字的最後一筆，因其格外拉長而引人注目（）。可資對照的寫法，見天池山相關介紹。

div.head: Location
div.p: East Mount Shuyuan 書院東山 is another site in the Shandong central ranges. The mountain rises to the east of Dazhai Valley 大寨谷 (fig. ).

East Mount Shuyuan is situated 25 km to the southwest of the county seat of Pingyin 平陰, 48 km to the south-southeast of Liaocheng City 聊城市, and 22 km northwest of the county seat of Dongping 東平 (map in front endpaper). Its foot is located at an altitude of ca. 100 m above sea level. To date, there has been no modern activity at East Mount Shuyuan. Houses of the nearby Shuyuan Village have been built close to the mountain’s foot.

Geographically, East Mount Shuyuan is situated 116.2937° east and 36.0997° north WGS84. This equals 436420 E (easting) and 3995240 N (northing) on the meridian 50S UTM. The inscription is found a few meters above the foot of the west side of East Mount Shuyuan, facing west-southwest. To the north, East Mount Shuyuan merges into Mount Tianchi 天池山, which carries inscriptions on its south side, facing southwest (map, fig. ). For the spatial relation of East Mount Shuyuan’s inscription to other inscription sites in Shandong, see the relevant introduction to Mount Tianchi.

div.head: Topography and Spatial Grouping
div.p: The single inscription at East Mount Shuyuan is situated close to the village, on the lower part of the steeply rising western slope of the East Mount (fig. ).

div.head: Place Name
div.p: Since the mountain is located east of Shuyuan Village in Hongfanchi Township 洪範池鎮 in Pingyin County 平陰縣, the villagers call it “East Mount”, Dongshan 東山.

div.head: Related Monasteries
div.p: See the relevant introduction on Mount Tianchi 天池山.

div.head: Related Persons
div.p: No information available.

div.head: Dating
div.p: As, apart from the Buddha name, no other inscriptions have been preserved, no exact dating is available for this site. Stylistically, the calligraphy of the Buddha name matches that of other carved Buddha names in Shandong that can be dated to the Northern Qi and Northern Zhou dynasties.

div.head: Research History
div.p:
The inscription of East Mount Shuyuan is mentioned several times in the second volume on Research on the Sutras Engraved on Polished Cliffs of the Northern Dynasties, vol. 2 (Beichao moya kejing yanjiu [xu]
北朝摩崖刻經研究［續］) published in 2003. 4; 104 and 120; 51 and 74; 180. Lai Fei’s monograph of 2007 gives full information on the inscription. .

div.head: Inventory
div.p: At East Mount Shuyuan, one Buddha name (SY 1) was carved.

div.head: SY 1
div.p: For the meaning of the name “Buddha King of Great Emptiness” see the relevant introduction on Mount Hongding’s 洪頂山 Buddha names. For all occurrences of this name in Shandong Province see HDS 8, discussion.

div.head: Carving Techniques
div.p: The technique is mainly “U”-shaped carving. The carver chisels in one movement along the middle of the stroke, which is convenient and fast.

div.head: Style of Calligraphy
div.p: To prepare the rock for carving, its surface was partially smoothened. The space in between and around the strokes of the characters still show traces of this process. The characters themselves are carved in the style of the Northern Qi dynasty. The last stroke of the character for “Buddha” 佛 catches the eye because of its outstanding length (fig. ). For comparative examples see the relevant introduction to Mount Tianchi 天池山.
